requirements.description: The worker fake surfaces a bounded startup outcome.
Provider defaults and resolved launch semantics materialize into the startup command for canonical worker profiles.
Resolved workdir, env, and startup hints survive templateParamsToConfig into runtime.Config.
A configured initial_message is injected into the first start exactly once.
A resumed session does not replay the initial_message after the first start has been recorded.
Schema overrides and initial_message handling preserve provider default launch flags while separating first-input delivery from option overrides.
A resumed session with in-progress assigned work receives a restart turn instead of landing idle when no explicit nudge is configured.
A resumed session with pre-claim work demand receives a restart turn instead of landing idle when no explicit nudge is configured.
Malformed or torn transcript data is reported as degraded history or a fail-closed load error instead of clean normalized history.
The standalone fake worker surfaces a blocked structured interaction signal and state.
Required structured interactions surface through the runtime interaction seam.
Responding to a pending structured interaction clears the pending state.
A mismatched interaction response is rejected without clearing the pending interaction.
Tmux interaction dedup state is instance-local so one worker session does not suppress another.
Required structured interactions are represented durably in normalized history and the pending transcript tail.
Dismissed and resumed-after-restart interaction lifecycle records update normalized tail state deterministically.
Normalized history preserves tool_use/tool_result substrate events.
Open tool_use events remain visible at the normalized transcript tail when unresolved.
A non-certifying production tmux runtime proof launches the canonical profile config and delivers initial input through the real transport path.
